markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Quicksilver Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3319)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You become swifter and nimbler but also fragile. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks, Reflex saves, and Dexterity-based attack rolls, and you gain the listed status bonus to your Speed. 

**Drawback** You take damage equal to twice your level; you can't recover Hit Points lost in this way by any means while the mutagen lasts. You take a –2 penalty to Fortitude saves.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Quicksilver Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +1, the bonus to Speed is +5 feet, and the duration is 1 minute.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 3"
pfs="Standard"
>
Quicksilver Mutagen (Moderate)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 12 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +2, the bonus to Speed is +10 feet, and the duration is 10 minutes.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Quicksilver Mutagen (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +3, the bonus to Speed is +15 feet, and the duration is 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Quicksilver Mutagen (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 549

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus to rolls is +4, the bonus to Speed is +20 feet, and the duration is 1 hour.
name: Quicksilver Mutagen

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silvertongue Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3321)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your features become striking, and your voice becomes musical and commanding, though emotion clouds your reason. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. Your critical failures with any of these skills become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks. Choose one skill in which you're trained; for the duration, you become untrained in that skill.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Silvertongue Mutagen (Lesser)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 550

**Price** 4 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The bonus is +1, and the duration is 1 minute.

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

Your features become striking, and your voice becomes musical and commanding, though emotion clouds your reason. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. Your critical failures with any of these skills become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks. Choose one skill in which you're trained; for the duration, you become untrained in that skill.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Silvertongue Mutagen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3321)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Elixir" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=589" />
<trait label="Mutagen" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=808" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=670" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 290

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Your features become striking, and your voice becomes musical and commanding, though emotion clouds your reason. 

**Benefit** You gain an item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40), and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. Your critical failures with any of these skills become failures instead. 

**Drawback** You take a –2 penalty to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks. Choose one skill in which you're trained; for the duration, you become untrained in that skill.

item_bonus_note: Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Wounds, or use Battle Medicine
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Skinstitch Salve](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3359)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=623" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 296

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sticky salve stubbornly holds wounds closed and encourages swift natural healing. You can activate the salve in either of the following ways.

**Activate—Administer Stitch** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) without requiring healer's tools. You can either gain a +2 item bonus to the Medicine check or use the salve's Medicine modifier of +13 instead of your own.

**Activate—Stitch Wounds** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You gain a +2 item bonus to the triggering Medicine check. If you roll a success on the Medicine check, you get a critical success instead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Skinstitch Salve

item_bonus_note: Religion checks to Recall Knowledge.
markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A large, stylized symbol of an eye adorns the top of this wooden staff, representing the watchful eye of the divine powers. The bearer of the staff can guide and protect, seeing bounties and tragedies that could befall them in the future. When wielding this staff, you gain a +1 item bonus to Survival checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) or [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) and to Religion checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. Staff of Providence **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 230 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549)
  • **1st** [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [_create water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476)
  • **2nd** [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [_create food_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1475), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [_status_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1690)
Staff of Providence (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 900 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **3rd** [_safe passage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659), [_wanderer's guide_](/Spells.aspx?ID=368)
  • **4th** [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [_dispelling globe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), _status_
Staff of Providence (Major) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 4,100 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **5th** _dispelling globe_, [_scouting eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661), _see the unseen_
  • **6th** _dispelling globe_, [_scintillating safeguard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=713), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)
Staff of Providence (True) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 64,000 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **7th** _dispelling globe_, [_energy aegis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1512)
  • **8th** _dispelling globe_, [_divine inspiration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1497)
  • **9th** _dispelling globe_, [_foresight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537), [_overwhelming presence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1621)
name: Staff of Providence

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alchemist Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3431)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 605

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These brass goggles are engraved with flame patterns and have thick, heavy lenses. While worn, they give you a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) alchemical items. When making [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with alchemical bombs, you ignore lesser [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) . If your Strike with an alchemical bomb fails (but doesn't critically fail), you gain a +1 item bonus to the splash damage the target of the Strike takes.

</column>

    pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These brass goggles are engraved with flame patterns and have thick, heavy lenses. While worn, they give you a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) alchemical items. When making [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with alchemical bombs, you ignore lesser [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) . If your Strike with an alchemical bomb fails (but doesn't critically fail), you gain a +1 item bonus to the splash damage the target of the Strike takes.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fire-Jump Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3433)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

**Price** 940 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black, metal ring is inset with rubies that occasionally give off wisps of smoke. It grants you a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks.

**Activate—Fire Jump** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2229), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You Stride (or Burrow or Fly, if you have the corresponding Speed) into any fire large enough to contain you, including magical fires. You vanish into the fire and take no damage from it. You can sense all sufficiently large fires within 100 feet of where you vanish, and you reemerge from any of those fires, either within the fire or adjacent to it. If you end your movement in the fire, it affects you as normal.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fire-Jump Ring

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Guise of the Smirking Devil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3434)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=541" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This ice-blue half-mask is adorned with a wicked silver grin that covers only the wearer's mouth. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks.

**Activate—Hideous Wail** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The mask emits a soul-chilling scream that deals 6d10 void damage to each living creature in a 20-foot emanation (DC 25 basic Fortitude save). 

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 9"
pfs="Standard"
>
Guise of the Smirking Devil
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Guise of the Smirking Devil (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

**Price** 35,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Intimidation is +3. Activating a _greater guise of the smirking devil_ casts [_wails of the damned_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1747) (DC 41), affecting all living creatures in the area.
name: Guise of the Smirking Devil

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Helm of Zeal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3435)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elaborate helmet is emblazoned with the divine symbols of a deity chosen when the helmet was crafted. You gain a +2 item bonus to that deity's Divine Skill.

**Activate—Rally to the cause** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a devotion spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Activate—Divine Fervor** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You've just used your champion's reaction;  **Effect** You gain an additional reaction you can use only for your champion's reaction. You lose this reaction if you don't use it by the start of your next turn. 

**Craft Requirements** You're a champion of the deity represented by the helmet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helm of Zeal
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Helm of Zeal (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

**Price** 21,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to the deity's Divine Skill is +3. You can activate Divine Fervor once per hour.
name: Helm of Zeal

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Helm of Zeal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3435)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elaborate helmet is emblazoned with the divine symbols of a deity chosen when the helmet was crafted. You gain a +2 item bonus to that deity's Divine Skill.

**Activate—Rally to the cause** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a devotion spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Activate—Divine Fervor** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You've just used your champion's reaction;  **Effect** You gain an additional reaction you can use only for your champion's reaction. You lose this reaction if you don't use it by the start of your next turn. 

**Craft Requirements** You're a champion of the deity represented by the helmet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</traits>

<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elaborate helmet is emblazoned with the divine symbols of a deity chosen when the helmet was crafted. You gain a +2 item bonus to that deity's Divine Skill.

**Activate—Rally to the cause** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a devotion spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Activate—Divine Fervor** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You've just used your champion's reaction;  **Effect** You gain an additional reaction you can use only for your champion's reaction. You lose this reaction if you don't use it by the start of your next turn. 

**Craft Requirements** You're a champion of the deity represented by the helmet.

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elaborate helmet is emblazoned with the divine symbols of a deity chosen when the helmet was crafted. You gain a +2 item bonus to that deity's Divine Skill.

**Activate—Rally to the cause** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a devotion spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Activate—Divine Fervor** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You've just used your champion's reaction;  **Effect** You gain an additional reaction you can use only for your champion's reaction. You lose this reaction if you don't use it by the start of your next turn. 

**Craft Requirements** You're a champion of the deity represented by the helmet.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Prognostic Veil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3436)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gauzy purple veil is embroidered with symbols of divinatory significance. As your curse worsens, the veil ripples in an ever-increasing unseen wind. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks.

**Activate—Remember the Future** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a revelation spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Activate—Twist the Skeins of Fate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** oncer per day; **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet is about to attempt a saving throw; **Requirements** You have the [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=320) condition; **Effect** The ally gains a status bonus to the saving throw equal to the value of your cursebound condition. 

**Craft Requirements** You're an oracle.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Prognostic Veil
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Prognostic Veil (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

**Price** 21,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Religion checks is +3. You can activate Twist the Skeins of Fate for an attack roll or a saving throw.
name: Prognostic Veil

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ring of Maniacal Devices](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3437)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magic ring seems like simple tarnished brass, but it enhances your curiosity about traps and devices of all kinds. You can use an Interact action to pull a [thieves' toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) from the ring. This toolkit appears in your hand and any part of it folds back into the ring if they would leave your possession. They grant you a +2 item bonus to Thievery checks to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) and to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412), and the ring's insights grant you the same bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) and [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) snares and traps.

**Activate—Fireball Trap** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You create the effects of a [_rune trap_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=124) ritual containing [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530). You can have only one trapped rune from a ring of maniacal devices active at a time, even if you have multiple rings, and the rune disappears if you lose your investiture in the ring.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ring of Maniacal Devices
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 614

**Price** 1,175 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ring of Maniacal Devices (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 614

**Price** 21,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ring grants a +3 bonus. Activating it creates a rune trap containing your choice of either a 7th-rank [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559) or a 7th-rank [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530).
name: Ring of Maniacal Devices

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magic ring seems like simple tarnished brass, but it enhances your curiosity about traps and devices of all kinds. You can use an Interact action to pull a [thieves' toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) from the ring. This toolkit appears in your hand and any part of it folds back into the ring if they would leave your possession. They grant you a +2 item bonus to Thievery checks to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) and to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412), and the ring's insights grant you the same bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) and [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) snares and traps.

**Activate—Fireball Trap** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You create the effects of a [_rune trap_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=124) ritual containing [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530). You can have only one trapped rune from a ring of maniacal devices active at a time, even if you have multiple rings, and the rune disappears if you lose your investiture in the ring.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

name: Ring of Maniacal Devices (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sash of Prowess](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3439)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This humble sash can be worn around the waist or across the chest. A sash of prowess often bears a coloration or a pattern that represents the monastery in which you trained but can also sport religious symbology, such as the open hand of Irori. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) skill checks.

**Activate—Effortless Mastery** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You succeed at an Acrobatics or Athletics skill check; **Effect** You critically succeed instead.

**Activate—Reserves of Inner Strength** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a qi spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Craft Requirements** You're a monk who can cast qi spells.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Sash of Prowess
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

>
Sash of Prowess (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 13,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to both skills is +3.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloud Buns](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3459)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 105

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Tian Xia Origin

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

White, pillowy dough surrounds an interior containing minced meats, spices, and herbs. The dough is steamed with elemental magic to make it light and airy while keeping the meaty center moist. When you consume a bun, a set of small clouds form around your feet that grant you a [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) Speed of 30 feet or your speed, whichever is lower, for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

name: Cloud Buns (Greater)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sun Herald's Stylus](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3473)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 130

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This writing instrument was made from the spur of one of the giant divine roosters that heralds the presence of [Shizuru](/Deities.aspx?ID=315). While you hold it, you gain a +2 item bonus to Calligraphy Lore and [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks.

**Activate—Rejuvenating Ink** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (manipulate) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Drawing a circle on the ground with your stylus, you cast [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526) centered on yourself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sun Herald's Stylus

The persistent bleed damage ends.

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This _+2 greater striking [grievous](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2841) [sawtooth saber](/Weapons.aspx?ID=423)_ has a red hilt and a purple-black blade. Favored by Red Mantis assassins who enjoy using illusions to confound their targets, a crimson bluff constantly flickers and flashes, creating brief afterimages that can be quite distracting. If you use a crimson bluff as part of a gesture when you [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387), you gain a +2 item bonus to your Deception check. 

**Activate—Shimmer Step** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455) and then you Step or Stride. 

**Activate—Swap Places** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An enemy would hit you with a melee Strike; **Requirements** You are under the effects of _blur_; **Effect** You swiftly dodge to the side as a momentary image of yourself manifests to distract the foe. The triggering Strike uses the outcome for one degree of success worse than the result of the attack roll. The duration of your _blur_ effect is reduced by 2 rounds.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs=""
>
[Elysian Clairglass](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3493)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=689" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 103

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Used by soldiers who battle on the borderlands of Elysium, particularly in Gorum’s realm of Clashing Shore, these bronze spyglasses are somewhat plain-looking save for a single diamond set into the largest cylinder. An Elysian clairglass functions like a typical spyglass, but you can see twenty times farther while looking through it, rather than eight times farther. It also grants a +3 item bonus to Perception checks to notice details at a distance.

**Activate—Seek and Speak** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _Elysian clairglass_ casts [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465) and [_clairvoyance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466) on the area you are currently observing with it. As long as you're holding the _Elysian clairglass_, you can speak through the floating eye or ear created by its spells, causing your voice to manifest as a whisper that only one person in range of the spells can hear, as a booming voice that everyone in range of the spells can hear, or as any combination in between those two extremes.

**Activate—Pinpoint** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_pinpoint_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1633).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

These paired bracers of red leather appear as plain armbands on the outside but display the branded symbol of [Achaekek](/Deities.aspx?ID=325) on the inside. They grant the wearer a +1 item bonus to AC and saving throws, and a maximum Dexterity modifier of +5 as armor. You can affix talismans to _assassin's bracers_ as though they were light armor.

**Activate—Focus on the Doomed** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You designate one creature within 30 feet. You cast 4th-rank [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) on yourself, but the designated creature can still see you. The bracers enhance your appearance to the designated creature, making you appear more fearsome and unnerving, granting you a +1 item bonus to attempts to Feint or Demoralize the designated creature. The designated creature takes a –1 item penalty to saving throws against fear effects.

**Activate—Crushing Embrace** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You are grappling a creature; **Effect** You Strike the creature you are grappling with a melee weapon or unarmed attack. This Strike deals an additional 3d6 precision damage and gains the death trait. The body of a creature that is slain by Crushing Embrace is so gruesomely damaged that the creature's body cannot be affected by any effect that requires an intact body to function, such as the [_talking corpse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) spell.

>
[Mask of the Mantis](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3497)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Prey for Death</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Prey for Death](/Sources.aspx?ID=231) pg. 104

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn head

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The traditional headwear for the Red Mantis assassin is the _mask of the mantis_. Most of these masks are constructed from hard leather helmets that serve to obscure the assassin's identity and give them the appearance of possessing a mantis's head. Some variants, however, consist only of the mask itself, and are meant to be worn over the face. Regardless of the shape and style, all _masks of the mantis_ function the same.

 Members of the Vernai can use any mask of the mantis to enhance their frightening presence—something they often do simply to reveal their standing in the society without speaking a word, as detailed fully in the entry for Vernai's Ire activation below.

 While wearing a _mask of the mantis_, you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks.

**Activate—Enhance Vision** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast either [_darkvision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1481) or [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663) on yourself.

**Activate—Vernai's Ire** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))   **Requirements** You are a member of the Vernai; **Effect** You cause the mask of the mantis's eyes to shimmer with crimson light, granting yourself a far more harrowing visage. This light creates illumination equivalent to that of a candle. You can use this ability again to extinguish the frightening glow, but as long as Vernai's Ire remains active, you gain a +1 item bonus to Intimidation checks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

If the target of this spell is a creature for which you've accepted a contract to assassinate, you are considered to have seen the creature in person for the purposes of _pinpoint's_ requirements.

The shape and exact flavor of these chocolate-covered cookies vary slightly based on who prepares them and the language they are imbued with when crafted. Although named for their use in obfuscating communications, Diarra finds them useful for quite the opposite reason.

 After eating one of these treats, you can speak and understand the chosen language for the next hour. If you have the [Read Lips](/Feats.aspx?ID=5204) or [Sign Language](/Feats.aspx?ID=5213) feat, you can use that language with those feats. A _conspirator's cookie_ grants you a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393) in the chosen language and you can add your level even if untrained.

**Craft Requirements** You must know the chosen language

Visitors to the Echo Repository always emerge with one of these silver coins, stamped with the visage of a faceless queen, somewhere on their person. An _echo token_ carries a minute shard of the Echo Repository's mission to impart lost information. 

**Activate—Flip a Coin** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** When flipped, the coin disintegrates into a glittery mist. You learn and memorize one random fact about a specific type of [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) (such as Architecture Lore, Elf Lore, Astral Plane Lore) that you didn't previously know, chosen by the GM. The next time you attempt a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) on this type of Lore within the next year, you gain a +1 status bonus on the check. You can benefit from only one echo token at a time in this way; if you Flip another echo token, the Lore skill changes. However, the memorized fact will remain perfectly in your memory forever unless magically altered or removed.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 43

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Murrou crafted these feathers to assist in remembering information. A _mnemonic feather_ is an emu feather imbued with magical energies. You can place the _mnemonic feather_ in a book with an Interact action. As long as the _mnemonic feather_ remains within the book, you recall information contained within that book perfectly, as if you were reading the page, as long as the book is on the same plane as you. When you attempt to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about a topic contained within the book, you gain a +1 status bonus to the check. 

**Activate—Recite Mnemonic** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet attempts a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check involving the topic in the _mnemonic feather’s_ book;  **Effect** You mentally impart the book’s knowledge to your ally, giving them the benefit of the _mnemonic feather_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mnemonic Feather

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 0"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fake Blood Pack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3528)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 56

**Price** 1 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn under light armor or clothes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Adventurers have found a number of uses for these animal blood–filled bladders, which were originally used in theatrical productions. Whenever you take slashing or piercing damage with the fake blood pack under your clothes or armor, roll a DC 11 flat check. On a success, the blood pack is punctured. You or an ally can puncture the hidden pack intentionally with an Interact action. When faking an injury, a punctured blood pack grants a +2 item bonus to relevant Deception checks, such as to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) about being injured, for 4 hours after the pack has been punctured or until the blood is washed off, whichever comes first. Abilities that trigger when a creature deals bleed damage, that determine if a creature is bleeding, or are otherwise based on bleed damage don’t trigger or apply for blood from a fake blood pack, which might mean creatures with such abilities automatically realize the ruse.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fake Blood Pack

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Inspiring Spotlight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3531)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 56, [Pathfinder #152: Legacy of the Lost God](/Sources.aspx?ID=23) pg. 73

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

An _inspiring spotlight_ consists of a drum-shaped metal housing around several reflective plates. It has the capacity to cast a powerful, narrow beam of light to illuminate important moments or characters on stage. The portable version consists of an 18-inch-diameter lamp on a tripod that can be set up or broken down over the course of 10 minutes. The mounted version is typically 3 feet in diameter and affixed to a bracket above and behind the audience for indoor performances.

**Activate—Light It Up** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The _inspiring spotlight_ emits a 5-foot burst of bright magical light within 120 feet. If the burst intersects with an area of magical darkness, the _inspiring spotlight_ attempts to counteract the darkness with a +17 modifier. Creatures within the burst gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws and Charisma-based skill checks. The spotlight remains lit for 1 minute or until you Interact to turn it off. During this time any creature adjacent to the spotlight can move the burst up to 20 feet from the burst’s original position with an Interact action.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<traits>

level="2"
right="Item 15"
pfs="Standard"
>
Mounted Inspiring Spotlight
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 55

**Price** 6,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 8

</row>

</column>

---

The spotlight has a 240-foot range, the counteract modifier is +25, and the item bonus is +2.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Talented Tap Shoes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3535)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 57

**Price** 1,250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These stylish shoes were originally created by the Tappin’ Toes Troupe, a group of tap-dancing Taldan bards who achieved massive popularity for their line dance routines. Notoriously lazy when it came to practicing choreography, the troupe enchanted their footwear to enhance their agility. Upon retirement, the troupe sold off their shoe designs, and talented tap shoes have become popular among professional dancers ever since. While wearing the shoes, you gain a +2 item bonus to Acrobatics checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369) and [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370) an enemy’s space and to Performance checks using dance.

**Activate—Strut Your Stuff** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You click the toes of your _talented tap shoes_ on the ground, and for the next minute, whenever you succeed or critically succeed at a Reflex save to avoid a damaging effect, you can Stride half your Speed as a reaction. However, during this time, you take a –2 item penalty to Stealth checks to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Talented Tap Shoes

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon of False Wounds](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3536)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=629" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=727" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 57

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 or more hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These weapons consist mostly of illusions, except in the spots where the weapon would be held. A sword, for example, would consist only of a physical handle with the permanent illusion of a blade. _Weapons of false wounds_ come in all shapes and sizes to help accurately reenact both historic and theatrical combat without risk of injury. The most amazing property of these items is the fact the illusions become partially physical when interacting with other _weapons of false wounds_, allowing blades to clash and parry as they would in actual combat. As a _weapon of false wounds_ is designed specifically to interact with other illusions, paying attention to subtle changes in the weapon’s appearance can help alert the wielder to nearby illusions. While using this item, you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to disbelieve an illusion.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Weapon of False Wounds

>
[Uzunjati Storytelling Amulet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3560)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 67

**Price** 145 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This round, flat amulet can be made of metal, clay, or leather and is usually highly personalized with runes, sigils, lines of poetry, or a depiction of a storyteller at work. In Azimbye’s case, their gold-rimmed metal amulet boasts fine dwarven workmanship, and bears lines from one of the oldest epic poems of the legendary folk hero Kgalaserke on one side and a stylized portrait of a storytelling event in Ranage’s Circle on the other. While wearing the amulet, you gain a +1 item bonus to Performance checks.

**Activate—Enamoring Story** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** The perfect anecdote or story to impress your interlocutor comes floating to your memory.;  **Effect** You attempt to Make an Impression or Request, using a Performance check instead of a Diplomacy check.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Uzunjati Storytelling Amulet

>
[Krasovnatype](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3566)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Gadget" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=405" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 92

**Price** 15 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A krasovnatype (named after Dr. Krasovna Gerenevich) consists of a carefully prepared silvered glass plate charged by a Stasian coil. When you touch the front of the glass or hold it near a living creature within your reach, an image of that creature’s aura is imprinted on the glass. Once a krasovnatype has an image on it, you can’t use the glass plate on another creature. If you are trained in Occultism, you can look at the image and learn if the associated creature has innate, prepared, or spontaneous spellcasting, along with the tradition of that casting and the highest rank of spells they can cast. Referencing the image grants a +1 item bonus to any [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) check regarding the creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Krasovnatype

>
[Oathlamp of Accord](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3575)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=640" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=647" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 103

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This hooded lantern takes the form of a translucent prism. It functions as a normal [hooded lantern](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2730), except that it isn’t fueled by oil, but by oaths. While you’re holding the lantern, you gain a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks.

**Activate—Annouce Oath** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647))  **Trigger** You make a promise in good faith;  **Effect** The _oathlamp of accord_ sheds light without consuming fuel until the promise you made is broken or fulfilled. The GM adjudicates whether a spoken promise is broken or fulfilled. This light and the shutters to conceal it work as normal for a hooded lantern. Any creature in the light of the oathlamp becomes aware of the contents of the oath, along with who made it and how long ago.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

item_bonus_note: Disarm manufactured items and to Grapple
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Zarothrask's Contract](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3578)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Contract" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=329" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=662" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 106

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You’ve bargained for power with a gongorinan, but in return, you must avoid furthering demonic goals. You gain a +2 item bonus to Athletics checks to Disarm manufactured items and to Grapple. Once per day, the gongorinan can warp your body with animal features; you must attempt a DC 25 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2. When you recover from the sickened condition, your features revert to normal. The gongorinan will usually do this if you sin or aid a demon, even unintentionally.

**Activate—Gongorinan's Emergence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Stony tentacles burst out of your body, lashing at foes. Creatures in a 10-foot emanation take 6d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 mental damage (DC 25 basic Fortitude save); on a failure, the creature also becomes [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure) as parts of their anatomy temporarily warp into animal features. When a creature recovers from the sickened condition, its features revert to normal.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Zarothrask's Contract

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Three-Pillared Yang Na](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3580)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Convocation</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Rival Academies](/Sources.aspx?ID=235) pg. 108

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo represents an abstract tree whose trunk is made up of three lines of Tang text. Applied in a ritual involving jasmine, turmeric, and a blossom from a yang na tree, the tattoo provides three blessings. The first blessing keeps your mind still during negotiations, granting a +1 item bonus to Diplomacy checks.

**Activate—Second Blessing** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574))    **Effect** You learn the direction of the yang na tree that gave the blossom used in creating your tattoo. Most such trees are in Tang Mai, far to the west of the Inner Sea. 

**Activate—Third Blessing** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32))  **Trigger** You take spirit damage;  **Effect** You gain resistance 3 against that spirit damage.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Three-Pillared Yang Na

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Loaded Dice](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3582)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [NPC Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=236) pg. 26

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These dice are weighted on one side to ensure they always land with the desired number facing up. Loaded dice grant you a +1 item bonus to [Games Lore](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1333) checks to gamble with the dice.

 A creature can determine a die is loaded while handling it with a successful [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=690) DC 20 Perception check. Determining dice are loaded without handling the dice is more difficult, especially since smart cheaters switch loaded dice in and out so they don't suspiciously roll the same number over and over. In this case, an observer must succeed at a secret Perception check against the roller's Games Lore or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) DC to notice the switches or the patterns.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Mocker's Swazzle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3630)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 80

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A swazzle is a device made of two strips of metal bound with sinew and around a reed that, when held to the mouth, causes the user’s voice to take on a distinctive rasping tone. Swazzles are often used by street puppeteers to give their puppets’ voices a unique and memorable timber. A _mocker’s swazzle_, though, has a different use—rather than simply giving voices to troublemaking puppets, it helps a performer fight back against hecklers in a crowd.

**Activate—Heckle the Heckler** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** A creature within 30 feet of you attempts and fails to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) you;  **Effect** You quickly fire a retort back at the triggering creature, mocking its failed attempt in a way that makes it laugh at its own ineptitude; you cast [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) (DC 30 Will save) on the triggering creature. 

**Activate—Mocking Spell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You direct a quick bit of insulting mockery at a creature who can understand you that’s within earshot. If your next action is to cast a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) spell that targets only that creature, that creature takes a –1 item penalty to any saving throw against the spell and, regardless of the result of the saving throw, becomes [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mocker's Swazzle

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Spectacles of Discernment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3631)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 80

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Designed for a frequent sponsor of theatrical productions who liked to stay informed of all the off-stage drama, these silver opera glasses are affixed to a slender redwood handle. They can’t be worn on the face but must be raised to the eyes and held there, at which point the spectacles of discernment grant a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks to notice details at a distance.

**Activate—Reveal the Truth** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You raise the spectacles to regard a target creature through them; the target must be within 60 feet. You immediately gain the ability to speak and understand the language the target creature is currently speaking, or its native language if it’s not currently talking, for 24 hours. During this time, you can Interact with the spectacles of discernment to flip down a pair of supplementary lenses. When you do so, if you’re observing the creature you originally targeted, roll a secret [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) check with a counteract modifier of +23 against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [morph](/Traits.aspx?ID=657), or [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670) effect affecting the target, but only for the purpose of determining whether you see through the disguise or not.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Discernment

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stage Magician's Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3634)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 81

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black velvet cloak has a silvery blue iridescent lining. The cloak can be worn with either side facing out—switching from one side to the other requires two Interact actions. If these actions are both taken on the same turn, the act of removing and redonning the cloak doesn’t cause it to lose its [investiture](/Rules.aspx?ID=3163). When worn with the black velvet lining facing out, the _stage magician’s cloak_ grants a +2 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) checks. When worn with the silvery blue lining facing out, it grants a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) checks. 

**Activate—Now You See Me...** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Distracting colorful smoke blasts outward from the cloak in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), causing all creatures in the area to become [off‑guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of your next turn (DC 30 Will save). If you were previously [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), you lose those conditions. You become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round and may use the extra action to [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297), [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306), or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

**Activate—...Now You Don't** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** Harmless silver smoke issues from the cloak in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). You cast a 2nd-rank [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) on yourself and are transported, along with all items you’re wearing and holding, from your current space to an unoccupied space within 30 feet that you can see. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you’re carrying it in an extradimensional space—the transportation part of this effect fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Stage Magician's Cloak

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 13"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Tumbler's Belt](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3635)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #204: Stage Fright](/Sources.aspx?ID=245) pg. 81

**Price** 3,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn belt

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This sparkling purple belt was made for the youngest member of a family of jugglers and tumblers who traveled with the Crascondo Company. The thought was this would allow the child to safely participate with their elder siblings and cousins, but the belt found itself being “loaned” more and more often to dare-taking elders in the troupe. The _tumbler’s belt_ grants a +2 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks, and whenever you critically succeed at a check to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=2370), you gain a +10-foot item bonus to your [Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2894) until the end of your turn. While wearing the _tumbler’s belt_, you’re not [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) while you [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369).

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Land With Grace;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You're falling;  **Effect** Treat your fall as if it were 120 feet shorter. Regardless of whether you take damage or not from the fall, you land on your feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Tumbler's Belt
search_markdown:
<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Curtain Call **Source** [Pathfinder #205: Singer, Stalker, Skinsaw Man](/Sources.aspx?ID=246) pg. 84 **Price** 15,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364) --- A _sinew-song_ is an ivory violin bow that bears an odd string, one that vibrates on its own, creating the soft sound of violin music whenever it’s swung through the air. When used to play a violin, this ivory bow functions normally but grants a +3 item bonus to any [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check attempted as a result. It can also be used to mime playing a violin when no instrument is at hand, but in this case, it grants no item bonus to resulting Performance checks. A _sinew-song’s_ primary use is as a weapon—it can be wielded as if it were a _[+3 ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [keen](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2843) [ greater thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850) [ greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [ sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364)_. **Activate—Cutting Cadenza** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) once and then swipe the weapon through the air three times. Shimmering waves of sound slice out in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). All creatures in the area must attempt a DC 37 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes damage as if you successfully hit it with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) using a _sinew-song_, but all of the damage is sonic damage.
**Failure** As success, but the creature also takes 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Critical Failure** As success, but double the damage and the creature takes 4d6 persistent bleed damage.
name: Sinew-Song

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

This elegant toga is infused with inexhaustible energy to enjoy life possessed by [azatas](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=368). You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) 20 and become immune to [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66). When you [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) in the robes, you either increase your Constitution score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become deafened while wearing the toga. 

**Activate—Elysium's Grasp** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are exposed to an [inhaled](/Traits.aspx?ID=634) [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669);  **Effect** Purifying gusts of sweet-scented air swirl around you, making you immune to inhaled poisons for 1 minute.

**Activate—Elysium's Breath** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623))    **Effect** The air around the robe constantly circulates to keep you healthy. For 8 hours, you become immune to [diseases](/Rules.aspx?ID=2767) spread via inhalation, [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664) effects, and environmental effects that would prevent you from breathing (including being underwater or from being strangled).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 81

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [dragon](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=395)-themed half-mask helps you to navigate through darkness and to notice things from afar—such as helping to find seats in a theater after a show begins, or to pick up details from a show even if you’re seated in the back row! You gain a +3 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks and never take a penalty to Perception checks based on distance or weather. You gain [darkvision, or greater darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=2410) if you have darkvision. When you [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) in the mask you either increase your Wisdom score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) as long as you wear the mask.

**Activate—Dragons See the Truth** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail a saving throw against an [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) effect;  **Effect** The mask attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the triggering illusion with a counteract rank of 9 and a counteract modifier of +31. You then gain the effects of [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

>
[Golden Gloves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3647)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 82

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn gloves

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These golden-threaded gloves fit snuggly past the elbows, and imbue all other worn clothing with an aristocratic mien. The gloves grant you a +3 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) skill checks. When you [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) the gloves, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. This gives you additional trained skills and languages, as normal for increasing your Intelligence modifier. You must select skills and languages the first time you invest the gloves, and whenever you invest the same golden gloves, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 when invested in these gloves.

**Activate—Heaven's Wings** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You throw wide your arms, putting the golden gloves on full display and causing a blazing halo to form above your head. All enemies within a 40-feet [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must make a DC 41 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 minute (or [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 minute on critical failure). [Unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) creatures take a –2 item penalty to their roll. The halo then melts into your body and attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any one [affliction](/Rules.aspx?ID=2389) you are currently suffering of your choice with a counteract rank of 9 and a counteract modifier of +31.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 82

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Lightning crackles from the wings flanking this helm, which is emblazoned with images of valkyries in the service of benevolent deities. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and gain [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 20. When you [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) in the helm, you either increase your Strength score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 as long as you wear the helm.

**Activate—Path of the Pegasus** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _sacred valkyrie helm_ casts [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) to your specifications. When you start this activation, each creature you target with this spell mounts a conjured pegasus who then carries them through reality to arrive at the specified location; each affected creature experiences the 10-minute activation as a kaleidoscopic ride through strange worlds and realities atop a pegasus’s back.

**Activate—Storm's Arms** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The helm casts a 9th-rank [_weapon storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) to your specifications. At your option, all damage caused by this spell is [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) damage—in this option, the duplicated weapons created by the spell appear to be made of lightning.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sacred Valkyrie Helm

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Silvanshee Collar](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3649)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 82

**Price** 40,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _silvanshee collar_ appears as a collar of prismatic cloth affixed with a tiny bell, but once worn on the body or affixed to your hair, a Tiny [cat](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=376) wearing a similar (but nonmagical) collar appears at your feet. This cat is similar to a [silvanshee](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1055) [agathion](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=216), but its stats are determined as if you gained the [Pet](/Feats.aspx?ID=5186) general feat. When you first [invest](/Actions.aspx?ID=2799) the collar, you must name your silvanshee pet, then build its statistics as detailed in the Pet general feat. The silvanshee has a name (chosen by you) and a unique personality. Whenever you invest the same silvanshee collar, the silvanshee who appears is the same one you named and chose the first time. If you already have a pet, familiar, or other companion that uses the Pet feat, that pet can change its shape as an action to appear as a silvanshee agathion at will (while perhaps retaining some of your pet’s cosmetic traits at your option), but this doesn’t otherwise alter your existing pet’s statistics.

As long as you wear the silvanshee collar, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks and a +2 circumstance bonus to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391). When you invest it, you either increase your Charisma score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever would give you a higher score. If you are [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), you become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 as long as you wear the collar.

**Activate—Awww!** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You fail on an attempt to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) or Make an Impression; **Requirements** Your pet can observe you; **Effect** Your pet does something distracting or adorable at the precise moment you failed, allowing you to reroll the failed check and to use the result of your choice as the actual result.
