    pfs="Limited"
>
[Storied Skin](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3658)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

</traits>

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 80

**Price** 45 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

---

Your skin becomes a canvas that records history as you learn it. When you receive the tattoo, choose a [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill. You can add the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait to the [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) action in order to study your tattoos, granting you a +1 item bonus to your check using the chosen Lore skill. A storied skin tattoo starts with an icon that represents a central event in your subject of study and is usually placed on the forehead or over the heart. Each time you learn about a major event in the history of that subject, an image, design, or symbol appears on your skin to represent the event.

**Activate—Living History** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per minute;   **Effect** The tattoo’s design animates for 1 round, crudely portraying some scene associated with the chosen Lore skill.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Needle of Undeath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3687)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #209: Destroyer's Doom](/Sources.aspx?ID=251) pg. 83

**Price** 1,150 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This thin, wand-like implement is carved from bone and scrimshawed with depictions of battle. While holding the needle of undeath, [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures see you as one of their own and are [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=82) to you until you take [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against them, and you have a +2 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks against intelligent undead. This does not affect a summoned undead’s attitude.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Speak with Undead;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You can use Diplomacy to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392) on mindless undead or make simple requests of them with a +2 item bonus. You cannot make this request of someone else’s summoned undead.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Boots of the Blight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3693)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 78

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn shoes

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Tanglebriar is not the only realm plagued by fiendish fungi. The soldiers of Nirmathas have long fought against a similar blight deep in Fangwood, and these boots are one of the more potent tools they’ve developed to help in this pursuit. Now that the Fangwood blight has been mostly contained, many of these boots have been gifted to elven soldiers, for they are equally useful to those who operate within Tanglebriar’s borders. They are also regarded as great trophies for the [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) and cultists who dwell within Tanglebriar, for not only can they make use of these boots’ powers, but wearing something created by your enemy gives [Treerazer’s](/Deities.aspx?ID=324) agents yet another way to engage in psychological warfare—they often adorn their boots with severed elf ears, or worse! 

These leather boots remain covered in mud no matter how often they are cleaned. The oiled leather resists water and keeps the feet and legs dry even when wading through water, but the boots retain a damp, fungal smell reminiscent of rotting vegetation. While wearing the boots, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=2374) or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) and to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Balance](/Actions.aspx?ID=2369).

**Activate—Swift Sidestep** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** You are about to make a Reflex saving throw against an [environmental hazard](/Rules.aspx?ID=2588) or [terrain feature](/Rules.aspx?ID=1791);  **Effect** The boots help you to avoid the hazard or effect, granting you a +2 status bonus to your saving throw. If you succeed at the saving throw, you become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 minute, but can use the additional action only to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

**Activate—Fungal Stride** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You ignore the effects of [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10 to damage caused by hazardous terrain. This activation lasts for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Skirmisher's Coat](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3696)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #210: Whispers in the Dirt](/Sources.aspx?ID=253) pg. 79

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Valued among soldiers (particularly those from wartorn Nirmathas) who often find themselves operating undercover, this large-collared garment is reminiscent of a highwayman’s coat. A skirmisher’s coat is cleverly constructed, and contains several concealed pockets and grants the wearer a +2 item bonus to checks made to [Conceal an Object](/Actions.aspx?ID=2403).

**Activate—Costume Change** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629))    **Effect** You change the shape and appearance of all your clothing, making them appear as ordinary or fine clothes of your imagining. This doesn’t change the statistics of any armor you’re wearing. Only a creature that’s benefiting from [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) or a similar effect can attempt to disbelieve this illusion, with a DC of 28.

**Activate—Unexpected Armament** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You reach into one of the coat’s pockets and withdraw a chunk of metal that instantly expands into a common simple or martial melee weapon of your choice. The weapon functions as a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_. When you produce the weapon, you decide if it is made from [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920), and which one of the following property runes it gains: [corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834), [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838), [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839), [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847), or [thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850). This weapon remains for 1 hour or until it leaves your grasp, at which point it vanishes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

**Activate—Unexpected Armament** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You reach into one of the coat’s pockets and withdraw a chunk of metal that instantly expands into a common simple or martial melee weapon of your choice. The weapon functions as a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) weapon_. When you produce the weapon, you decide if it is made from [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920), and which one of the following property runes it gains: [corrosive](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2834), [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838), [frost](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2839), [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847), or [thundering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2850). This weapon remains for 1 hour or until it leaves your grasp, at which point it vanishes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Skirmisher's Coat

The kora grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks made to tell stories when used to accompany song or speech. _The kora of the unending story_ is a coda instrument—an item that functions like a [staff](/Weapons.aspx?ID=367) but in the form of a [musical instrument](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2737). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the kora to cast a spell from its list.

  • **1st** [_object reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2012)

  • **3rd** [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[What Doors We Open](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3705)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Spore War</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #211: The Secret of Deathstalk Tower](/Sources.aspx?ID=254) pg. 83

**Price** 4,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_What Doors We Open_ is the culmination of Aravashnial’s research. Before he was captured, he performed a 1-minute ritual to erase his spells from this book and then hid it at his campsite to prevent his [demonic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387) foes from learning too many of his secrets. A spellcaster can transfer their own spells to this grimoire using a similar 1-minute ritual. When a spellcaster prepares their spells from it, they gain the ability to Activate the grimoire until their next [daily preparations](/Rules.aspx?ID=2575) and gain a +2 item bonus to checks made to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about demons. A grimoire’s benefits apply only to spells cast via spell slots. No one can use more than one grimoire per day, nor can a grimoire be used by more than one person per day.

**Activate—Cast Out** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If the next activity you take this round is to cast [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448) prepared from this grimoire, it counts as the extra cost of adding an object that is anathema to the creature and gives the creature a –2 circumstance penalty to its save. If the targeted creature is a [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=573), increase this to a –3 circumstance penalty.

**Activate—Open the Door Within** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The inside back cover of _What Doors We Open_ features a realistic depiction of a bejeweled door set in a stone wall. When you Open the Door Within, you open that door to reveal an extradimensional space that otherwise functions identically to a [type II spacious pouch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3032).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: What Doors We Open

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Cleft Head Marking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3739)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #207: Resurrection Flood](/Sources.aspx?ID=249) pg. 79

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Caution and misdirection are the hallmarks of your hold. You’ve encountered more travelers than most of Belkzen’s holds ever see, and you need to understand how to deal with them. You’re a proud member of the Cleft Head Hold or have been found worthy of wearing their mark. This tattoo is a crooked line that begins high above one eye and zigzags toward your mouth. The tattoo’s grim appearance can help mask your true motivations. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=1434).

**Activate—Unexpected Strike** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) a creature that has the [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) condition with a weapon attack;  **Effect** You deal an extra 1d6 precision damage to the target.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 4"
pfs="Limited"

</title>

<traits>

**Price** 850 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus is +2, and Unexpected Strike deals 2d6 precision damage.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Stormshard](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3742)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Triumph of the Tusk</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #208: Hoof, Cinder, and Storm](/Sources.aspx?ID=250) pg. 78

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These shards of coalesced necromantic essence are sometimes found in the wake of an ancestor storm. Howling spirits are faintly visible, trapped inside the jagged, dark-green glass.

You can use the shard to cow the [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) or briefly release the tormented souls from inside the glass. While you hold the stormshard, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [influence](/Rules.aspx?ID=1830) the undead. In addition, you can [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) undead creatures, even if those creatures are [mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) or otherwise [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) to [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), or [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects.

**Activate—Free the Spirits** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You briefly release the spirits from the _stormshard_ before drawing them back into the glass. Each creature in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 4d8 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Fortitude save). You treat the result of your saving throw as one degree of success better than its outcome.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 21"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[White Cleome's Eye](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3759)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Curtain Call</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #206: Bring the House Down](/Sources.aspx?ID=247) pg. 83

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Delicate engravings of spiders and cleomes decorate the silver cover of this magnifying loupe. _White Cleome’s Eye_ functions as [_spectacles of inquiry_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2316), but the item bonuses granted to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks increase by 1 (so +3 while worn, and +4 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) when you activate the artifact).

Once restored, _White Cleome’s Eye_ gains the following additional activations.

**Activate—Look to the Past** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twist _White Cleome’s Eye_ in a clockwise rotation, allowing you to peer back in time, causing it to cast a 9th-rank [_retrocognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) spell.

**Activate—Relive the Past** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twist _White Cleome’s Eye_ in a counter-clockwise rotation, allowing you to experience previous events that impacted an object you are touching, causing _White Cleome’s Eye_ to cast a 9th-rank [_object reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2012) spell.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: White Cleome's Eye

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Twilight Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3765)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 52

**Price** 125 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

**Access** You’re a member of the Twilight Talons.

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This tattoo of a black eagle gripping a sword and arrows in its talons identifies the bearer as a member of the Twilight Talons. Agents typically keep the tattoo hidden unless they need to prove their identity to another member. The bearer gains a +1 item bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks.

**Activate—Fade** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629))    **Effect** You hide your tattoo from view. The tattoo is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) for 1 day and can’t be detected by effects such as [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) and [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646). You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this effect. 

**Activate—Inscribe** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You lay your hand on a piece of text, and your tattoo makes a perfect copy of it, storing it as a ring of swirling letters surrounding the design. The tattoo can hold text equivalent to two pages of a book, a single [scroll](/Traits.aspx?ID=688), or a similar area of other surfaces, though it doesn’t replicate any magical effect or other special properties of the original words. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this effect, and when you Dismiss it, the tattoo copies the original text onto a blank writing surface you’re touching.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Twilight Tattoo

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Emperor's Peak Quartz Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3766)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 100

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

**Price** 5 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

While most residents of—and travelers to—the Five Kings Mountains live underground, a significant number of people explore the regions’ awe-inspiring peaks. Among their many discoveries on Emperor’s Peak is a deposit of rainbow-colored rock crystal quartz with inherent magical properties that aid in survival, especially in the mountains. Dwarven artisans fashion chunks of this translucent quartz into fashionable bracelets. Wearing such a bracelet grants you a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) and [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368). This bonus increases to +3 when in mountainous [terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2365). If you attempt a Survival check to Subsist after 8 hours or less of exploration, you take only a –2 penalty instead of a –5 penalty.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Emperor's Peak Quartz Bracelet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Memory Ribbon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3768)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 101

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

**Price** 20 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The time-honored tradition of weaving beautiful, embroidered glory ribbons throughout one’s hair and beard remains an important cultural practice among some dwarven clans, with the choice of colors and style of presentation representing status, achievements, and other significant aspects of someone’s position. Magical versions also exist that help enhance the wearer’s memory. These magical ribbons are especially popular when someone has been invited to serve as a toastmaster at a guild banquet or as a master of ceremonies at an important festival. Their use in final oral exams for high positions is, however, hotly debated.

**Activate—Read the Ribbon's Story** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You attempt a skill check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) but haven’t rolled yet;  **Effect** The _memory ribbon_ grants you a +2 item bonus to the triggering skill check to Recall Knowledge. Afterward, the ribbon becomes non-magical.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Memory Ribbon

</title>

<traits>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kols's Oath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3770)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 101

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This filigree grants you, as the clan dagger’s owner, a +1 item bonus to [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) checks and to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=2393). Additionally, once per day, the filigree symbol can be activated to compel an enemy to act.

**Activate—Vow Unbreakable** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You command a creature within 30 feet to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) away from you, [drop prone](/Actions.aspx?ID=2295), or [release](/Actions.aspx?ID=2300) one item it’s holding. The creature can choose to perform that action as the first action on its next turn; if it doesn’t, it takes 4d6 mental damage (DC 20 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2247) Will save).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 5"
pfs="Standard"
>
Kols's Oath
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 101

**Price** 160 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Greater Kols's Oath
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 101

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The bonuses increase to +2, the damage increases to 8d6, and the DC increases to 28.
name: Kols's Oath

>
[Trudd's Strength](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3771)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 101

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Five Kings Mountains have access to this option._

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This filigree depiction of a warhammer in front of a kiteshaped shield grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks and to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394). Additionally, once per day, the filigree symbol can be activated to protect your allies.

The bonus lasts for 1 minute.

item_bonus_note: item bonus to Diplomacy checks to Make an Impression
markdown:
**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 149 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Characters from Kyonin have access to this option. When using a _shawl of seasons_, the season is the current season where the player is located._ **Price** 150 gp **Usage** worn **Bulk** L --- This woven shawl changes its color, material, and abilities with the seasons. In its usual state, it reflects the current season of the environment the wearer is in. The handsome embroidery grants you a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392). **Activate—Change of Seasons** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You arrange the shawl on your shoulders to gain the benefits of a season of your choice until your next daily preparations. If the season you choose is the current season, you also gain a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws.
  • **Spring** The shawl becomes smooth silk covered in delicate flowers. You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed.
  • **Summer** The shawl becomes light cotton with the hues of fresh grass. You’re protected from the effects of [severe heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820).
  • **Fall** The shawl becomes thin leather with an ombre of red and orange leaves. You treat [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352) as 10 feet shorter.
  • **Winter** The shawl becomes warm wool as white as snow. You’re protected from the effects of severe cold.
name: Shawl of Seasons

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jaws of the Grogrisant](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3780)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 173

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn circlet

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The teeth that fell off the [_Mantle of the Grogrisant_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1476) were given to the scholars of Houses Fahlspar, Lotheed, Nicodemius, and Zespire, who—after much heated discussion—created this regal circlet. You gain a +3 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) and [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) skill checks and [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) checks against creatures that have the [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) trait. When you invest in the headband, you either increase your Wisdom modifier by 1 or increase it to +4, whichever would give you a higher value.

**Activate—Primal Empathy** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You gain the ability to communicate with nature as if you were a part of it. You cast [_telepathy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1719) at 6th rank, which can only be used to communicate with creatures that have the [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Jaws of the Grogrisant

item_bonus_note: Item Bonus
markdown:
May contain spoilers from Shades of Blood **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 79 **Bulk** 20 --- An _essence forge_ consists of a large stone workbench with a receptacle for fuel built into the left side. Sparkling crystals are embedded across the forge’s structure, with additional flourishes made from a different material, as appropriate to the type of forge. _Life forges_ are adorned with fossilized bones and petrified wood. They’re used to craft items associated with the [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) and [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) magic traditions. One must attempt a [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check to prime a life forge. _Matter forges_ are decorated with strips of precious metal. They can be used to craft items associated with the [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) and primal magic traditions. One must attempt an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) or Nature check to prime a matter forge. _Mind forges_ are accentuated with glittering gemstones. They can be used to craft items associated with the arcane and [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) magic traditions. One must attempt an Arcana or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check to prime a mind forge. _Spirit forges_ are emblazoned with strange runes and obscure symbols enhanced by different pigments. They can be used to craft items associated with the divine and occult magic traditions. One must attempt an Occultism or Religion check to prime a spirit forge. **Activate—Prime the Forge** 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The _essence forge_ doesn’t currently contain any stored essence; **Effect** You prepare the _essence forge_ for use by placing raw materials in its receptacle then spend an hour concentrating on the forge, using your own magical potential to consume the materials. The combined gp value of the raw materials sets the maximum price of the item you can craft with the _essence forge_. An _essence forge’s_ receptacle can hold no more than 3 Bulk of raw materials, so coins, gemstones, or small valuable objects or items make for the best sources of fuel. Attempt an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check as appropriate for the tradition of essence forge you’re using to focus your magical potential on the receptacle; the DC for this check depends on the type of forge (lesser, moderate, or greater) you’re using. **Critical Success** The raw materials are consumed, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to the value of the raw materials. Additional essence overflows back into you, granting an item bonus to the skill you used to Prime the Forge for the next 24 hours. The size of the item bonus depends on the type of the forge.
**Success** The raw materials are consumed, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to the value of the raw materials.
**Failure** The raw materials are consumed, but the burn is inefficient, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to half the value of the raw materials.
**Critical Failure** The raw materials are consumed and lost, but no essence is stored in the forge; the raw materials are wasted. **Activate—Craft an Item** 2 hours ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The _essence forge_ has been primed with raw materials equaling or exceeding the value o f the item you intend to craft, and you are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73); **Effect** You place your hands atop the forge’s workspace and focus your concentration on the essence contained within the forge. Choose an item whose formula you have and whose level is equal to or less than the essence forge’s level, or choose an [_essence charm_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3795). The _essence forge_ begins to construct that item as you concentrate, causing an increasingly realistic illusion of the item to come into focus atop the forge’s workspace. Attempt a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check with a DC determined by the forge’s type (lesser, moderate, or greater); this check earns Forge Points (using the [Victory Point system](/Rules.aspx?ID=3028)). You can sustain this activation for up to 8 hours; each time you sustain the activation, attempt another Crafting check to earn Forge Points. When you choose to end the activation, you become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and any remaining essence stored in the forge dissipates with no further effect. Consult the sidebar to determine if you were successful in crafting the item you chose. **Activate—Absorb Essence** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The _essence forge_ contains essence; **Effect** You absorb the essence in the forge, emptying it and allowing it to be primed. If the essence value in the forge is equal to or greater than the value determined by the forge’s type, the forge heals you for its type’s listed HP. Otherwise the absorbed essence has no effect. Essence Forge (Lesser) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 250 gp --- DC 20 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +1 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 50 gp, the forge restores 3d8+10 Hit Points Essence Forge (Moderate) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 2,000 gp --- DC 30 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +2 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 400 gp, the forge restores 6d8+20 Hit Points. Essence Forge (Greater) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 24,000 gp --- DC 40 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +3 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 5,000 gp, the forge restores 8d8+30 Hit Points.
name: Essence Forge (Lesser)

One must attempt an Occultism or Religion check to prime a spirit forge. **Activate—Prime the Forge** 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The _essence forge_ doesn’t currently contain any stored essence; **Effect** You prepare the _essence forge_ for use by placing raw materials in its receptacle then spend an hour concentrating on the forge, using your own magical potential to consume the materials. The combined gp value of the raw materials sets the maximum price of the item you can craft with the _essence forge_. An _essence forge’s_ receptacle can hold no more than 3 Bulk of raw materials, so coins, gemstones, or small valuable objects or items make for the best sources of fuel. Attempt an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check as appropriate for the tradition of essence forge you’re using to focus your magical potential on the receptacle; the DC for this check depends on the type of forge (lesser, moderate, or greater) you’re using. **Critical Success** The raw materials are consumed, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to the value of the raw materials. Additional essence overflows back into you, granting an item bonus to the skill you used to Prime the Forge for the next 24 hours. The size of the item bonus depends on the type of the forge.
**Success** The raw materials are consumed, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to the value of the raw materials.
**Failure** The raw materials are consumed, but the burn is inefficient, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to half the value of the raw materials.
**Critical Failure** The raw materials are consumed and lost, but no essence is stored in the forge; the raw materials are wasted. **Activate—Craft an Item** 2 hours ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The _essence forge_ has been primed with raw materials equaling or exceeding the value o f the item you intend to craft, and you are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73); **Effect** You place your hands atop the forge’s workspace and focus your concentration on the essence contained within the forge. Choose an item whose formula you have and whose level is equal to or less than the essence forge’s level, or choose an [_essence charm_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3795). The _essence forge_ begins to construct that item as you concentrate, causing an increasingly realistic illusion of the item to come into focus atop the forge’s workspace. Attempt a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check with a DC determined by the forge’s type (lesser, moderate, or greater); this check earns Forge Points (using the [Victory Point system](/Rules.aspx?ID=3028)). You can sustain this activation for up to 8 hours; each time you sustain the activation, attempt another Crafting check to earn Forge Points. When you choose to end the activation, you become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and any remaining essence stored in the forge dissipates with no further effect. Consult the sidebar to determine if you were successful in crafting the item you chose. **Activate—Absorb Essence** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The _essence forge_ contains essence; **Effect** You absorb the essence in the forge, emptying it and allowing it to be primed. If the essence value in the forge is equal to or greater than the value determined by the forge’s type, the forge heals you for its type’s listed HP. Otherwise the absorbed essence has no effect. Essence Forge (Lesser) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 250 gp --- DC 20 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +1 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 50 gp, the forge restores 3d8+10 Hit Points Essence Forge (Moderate) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 2,000 gp --- DC 30 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +2 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 400 gp, the forge restores 6d8+20 Hit Points. Essence Forge (Greater) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 24,000 gp --- DC 40 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +3 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 5,000 gp, the forge restores 8d8+30 Hit Points.
name: Essence Forge (Moderate)

An _essence forge’s_ receptacle can hold no more than 3 Bulk of raw materials, so coins, gemstones, or small valuable objects or items make for the best sources of fuel. Attempt an [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check as appropriate for the tradition of essence forge you’re using to focus your magical potential on the receptacle; the DC for this check depends on the type of forge (lesser, moderate, or greater) you’re using. **Critical Success** The raw materials are consumed, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to the value of the raw materials. Additional essence overflows back into you, granting an item bonus to the skill you used to Prime the Forge for the next 24 hours. The size of the item bonus depends on the type of the forge.
**Success** The raw materials are consumed, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to the value of the raw materials.
**Failure** The raw materials are consumed, but the burn is inefficient, and the _essence forge_ stores essence equal to half the value of the raw materials.
**Critical Failure** The raw materials are consumed and lost, but no essence is stored in the forge; the raw materials are wasted. **Activate—Craft an Item** 2 hours ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The _essence forge_ has been primed with raw materials equaling or exceeding the value o f the item you intend to craft, and you are not [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73); **Effect** You place your hands atop the forge’s workspace and focus your concentration on the essence contained within the forge. Choose an item whose formula you have and whose level is equal to or less than the essence forge’s level, or choose an [_essence charm_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3795). The _essence forge_ begins to construct that item as you concentrate, causing an increasingly realistic illusion of the item to come into focus atop the forge’s workspace. Attempt a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) check with a DC determined by the forge’s type (lesser, moderate, or greater); this check earns Forge Points (using the [Victory Point system](/Rules.aspx?ID=3028)). You can sustain this activation for up to 8 hours; each time you sustain the activation, attempt another Crafting check to earn Forge Points. When you choose to end the activation, you become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), and any remaining essence stored in the forge dissipates with no further effect. Consult the sidebar to determine if you were successful in crafting the item you chose. **Activate—Absorb Essence** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The _essence forge_ contains essence; **Effect** You absorb the essence in the forge, emptying it and allowing it to be primed. If the essence value in the forge is equal to or greater than the value determined by the forge’s type, the forge heals you for its type’s listed HP. Otherwise the absorbed essence has no effect. Essence Forge (Lesser) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 250 gp --- DC 20 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +1 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 50 gp, the forge restores 3d8+10 Hit Points Essence Forge (Moderate) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 2,000 gp --- DC 30 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +2 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 400 gp, the forge restores 6d8+20 Hit Points. Essence Forge (Greater) **Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 80 **Price** 24,000 gp --- DC 40 check to use the forge. When you critically succeed at Priming the Forge, you gain a +3 item bonus, and when you Absorb Essence worth at least 5,000 gp, the forge restores 8d8+30 Hit Points.
name: Essence Forge (Greater)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Essence Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3795)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Shades of Blood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 81

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Anyone can use an [_essence forge_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3791) to craft an _essence charm_ without needing to know the formula for an _essence charm_. No larger than a coin, an _essence charm_ is normally worn around the neck or hung from a belt—its exact appearance is up to the crafter. When an _essence charm_ is created, its crafter selects a skill in which they are at least trained. The _essence charm_ grants a +1 item bonus to that skill when worn. 

**Activate—Lucky Charm** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail or critically fail a check using the _essence charm's_ chosen skill;  **Effect** A failed check becomes a success, and a critical failure becomes a failure.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Essence Charm

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Essence Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3795)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Shades of Blood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 81

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Anyone can use an [_essence forge_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3791) to craft an _essence charm_ without needing to know the formula for an _essence charm_. No larger than a coin, an _essence charm_ is normally worn around the neck or hung from a belt—its exact appearance is up to the crafter. When an _essence charm_ is created, its crafter selects a skill in which they are at least trained. The _essence charm_ grants a +1 item bonus to that skill when worn. 

**Activate—Lucky Charm** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail or critically fail a check using the _essence charm's_ chosen skill;  **Effect** A failed check becomes a success, and a critical failure becomes a failure.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Essence Charm

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Essence Charm](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3795)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Shades of Blood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #214: The Broken Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=258) pg. 81

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Anyone can use an [_essence forge_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3791) to craft an _essence charm_ without needing to know the formula for an _essence charm_. No larger than a coin, an _essence charm_ is normally worn around the neck or hung from a belt—its exact appearance is up to the crafter. When an _essence charm_ is created, its crafter selects a skill in which they are at least trained. The _essence charm_ grants a +1 item bonus to that skill when worn. 

**Activate—Lucky Charm** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You fail or critically fail a check using the _essence charm's_ chosen skill;  **Effect** A failed check becomes a success, and a critical failure becomes a failure.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Essence Charm

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Shades of Blood **Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 79 **Price** 360 gp **Usage** worn --- When displayed prominently, this iron badge grants you authority and gravitas. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks. **Activate—Authoritative Command** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Effect** You shout at a foe within 60 feet, compelling them to stand in place and drop everything they’re holding. The target attempts a DC 23 Will save with the following results. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as it does one of the following at the beginning of its next turn: stand in place or release what it’s holding.
**Failure** The target is slowed 2 as it stands in place and releases what it’s holding at the beginning of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** The target is slowed 3 as it spends its next turn to stand in place, release what it’s holding, and place its hands in the air. **Special** A creature wearing an invested security badge within En-Gokal gains additional benefits, as noted in the [adventure text](/Sources.aspx?ID=258).

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Schematic Scanner](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3800)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Shades of Blood</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #215: To Blot Out the Sun](/Sources.aspx?ID=259) pg. 80

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _schematic scanner_ is a complex brass device featuring multiple mounted lenses, clamps, and apertures. A _schematic scanner_ can hold the formulas for items as if it were a formula book with unlimited capacity. You can add a formula to the _schematic scanner_ using the Store Schematics activation. A _schematic scanner_ grants you a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks to craft an item whose formula is stored in the _schematic scanner_. 

**Activate—Store Schematics** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You place the written formula for an item into the _schematic scanner_ and view it through the mounted lenses, magically storing a copy of the formula inside the _schematic scanner_.

**Activate—Reverse Engineer Schematics** <actions string="" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You place an item in front of the _schematic scanner_, then view the item through the mounted lenses, magically learning the formula for the targeted item, without disassembling or causing harm to the item, and storing the formula in the _schematic scanner_. Reverse engineering a formula in this way takes 10 minutes and doesn’t cost any gold.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Schematic Scanner

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eagle Wing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3811)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 121

**Price** 950 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn armor

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Armor** [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Long black feathers cover the leather pieces of this _[+1 resilient ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_. The armor gives you the ability to glide safely to earth from high above the battlefield. It also grants you a +2 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks you attempt while in the air.

**Activate—Soar** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You glide slowly toward the ground, 5 feet down and 30 feet forward through the air. Provided you spend at least 1 action gliding on your turn and haven’t yet reached the ground, you remain in the air at the end of your turn. Otherwise, you fall.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Eagle Wing

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 14"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Medic's Shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3829)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 124

**Price** 4,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This [standard-grade dawnsilver shield](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2814) (Hardness 5, HP 20, BT 10) is inscribed with the symbol of a deity dedicated to [healing](/Domains.aspx?ID=78) and medicine. The shield is designed for combat medics to aid combatants or adventurers in the midst of battle. [Healer’s tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2727) can be stored on the backside of this shield. This shield grants a +2 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks.

**Activate—Adrenaline Boost** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You grant a medicinal boost to bring someone back into the fray. You [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) to stabilize an adjacent ally with the [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) condition. On a success, they gain 6d6+20 Hit Points, can immediately [Stand](/Actions.aspx?ID=2303) as a free action, and become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round. They can use this extra action only to [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Medic's Shield

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Doomsweeper](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3842)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 126

**Price** 475 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=381)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Functioning as a _[+1 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [halberd](/Weapons.aspx?ID=381)_ when wielded as a weapon, a _doomsweeper_ is a heavy steel rake-like implement commonly used by frontline soldiers to scour the battlefield for unseen dangers. When you hold a _doomsweeper_ extended in front of you, you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to notice any hidden hazards in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386), with the bonus increasing to +2 if you are performing the Scout or Search exploration action.

**Activate—Clear the Way** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You’re holding the _doomsweeper_ in 2 hands; **Effect** The _doomsweeper_ sweeps the ground clear of obstacles in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). This automatically removes any mundane effects of 4th level or lower that would hinder ground movement through the area of effect, including [caltrops](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2708) and non-magical [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) features no deeper or higher than 4 feet. It also attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) any magical dangers or obstacles with a +14 modifier and a counteract rank of 4th. Finally, it attempts a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) check with a +14 modifier to disable all non-magical hazards (but not haunts) in the area. You don’t need to be aware of any hazards or effects in the area for the _doomsweeper_ to remove them, but you don’t become aware of them if the attempt fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Doomsweeper

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Gravedigger's Call](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3846)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 127

**Price** 1,700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=375)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This rusted shovel functions as a _[+2 striking ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [decaying ](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2835) [glaive](/Weapons.aspx?ID=375)_ when wielded as a weapon, but it more closely resembles a neglected tool. Its haft was carved from the trunk of a long-dead tree that once grew among the broken stones of an abandoned cemetery, and its blade was forged from shattered remnants of the armor that failed to protect those buried there. When you carry or wield a gravedigger’s call, you gain a +1 item bonus to Perception checks to Seek haunts and to skill checks to determine the reasons for a haunt or spirit’s existence.

**Activate—Call the Fallen** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You cast [_rouse skeletons_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2019) as a 5th-rank [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) spell. The DC is 30.

**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of _rouse skeletons_ (5th rank).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Gravedigger's Call

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Atlas Arcane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3922)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 142

**Price** 350 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This well-worn vellum scroll has edges trimmed with golden thread, and it unrolls to reveal a map of the nearby area. The _atlas arcane_ always shows the surrounding area (out to a 36- mile radius centered on the map) with a reasonable level of detail, providing a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks and any skill checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Rules.aspx?ID=2638), provided the checks are related to the location detailed on the map.

**Activate—Situation Report** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You speak a command phrase, and the map reveals the location of all troop movements within the area it maps. This intel is current the moment the phrase is spoken but does not update afterward, and moving the map does not reveal further intel.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Atlas Arcane

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bellows Pipes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3925)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 142

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Smaller than breath-powered bagpipes, the uilleann pipes are worked using a set of elbow bellows, and the pipes are made from finely carved bone. This bagpipe grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument.

**Activate—Hand Chords** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You execute a complex set of complementary arpeggios for dramatic effect. You and all allies within a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain a +1 status bonus to the next attack roll, Perception check, saving throw, or skill check you attempt before the end of your next turn. Each target chooses which roll to use the bonus on before rolling.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bellows Pipes

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Benthic Drums](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3926)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 142

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large bass drum is made of driftwood and whale hide, with swirling shapes that hint at more distinct and sinister forms carved on its surface. This drum grants you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument. You can communicate basic ideas with whales and other large sea animals by playing music with this instrument.

**Activate—Call from the Depths** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You drum a song of an ancient creature, calling forth the cries of a great whale in the minds of your foes. All enemies in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 9d10 sonic damage (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail are also [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Benthic Drums

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Blakenshipper](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3927)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 142

**Price** 340 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This strange instrument is both played and worn, consisting of a set of pipes on a metal arm attached to a harness, a horn, a drum attached to a foot pedal, arm bellows, various keys, cymbals, and more esoteric elements. The blakenshipper grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument.

**Activate—Be the Band** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You work double-time, playing an entire band’s composition yourself, bolstering those around you by your mighty effort. You and all allies within a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain 15 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 round. For the next minute, you can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) to continue the music, granting you and all allies within a 60-foot emanation 5 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 round; this Sustain action gains the auditory and manipulate traits.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Blakenshipper

The blast deals 4d6 sonic damage to each creature in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). ---

This scepter is made from dragon bone, with minute runes carved along its length, a dragonskin leather grip, and caps of gold on each end. The scepter grants you a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395). Such items are considered particularly gruesome and vile by dragons, and they’re invariably hostile to any creature they discover carrying a _draconic verge_, going so far as to single out that individual for destruction.

**Activate—Dragon’s Eminence** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You hold the verge aloft, tapping into the majesty of the dragon from whom the verge was made. All enemies in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must succeed a DC 23 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 (frightened 3 on a critical failure).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"

>
[Feather of the Unfounded Bravado](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3935)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 144

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large feather is garishly colored in reds, oranges, and yellows, but on closer inspection, it’s simply a particularly large chicken feather that’s been dyed. Carrying the feather provides a boost to your self-confidence, but it might lead you into dangerous situations.

**Activate—Overconfident Facade** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** For 1 hour, the feather grants you a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) and [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=2392), but a –1 item penalty on [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks, as your inflated confidence leads you to attempt things you simply cannot do.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

[Fife of the Faithful](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3936)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 144

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This small metal fife is of extraordinary fine quality, with gold filigree on the embouchure. A _fife of the faithful_ grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument.

**Activate—Call to Arms** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You play a rousing tune on the fife that carries across the battlefield. You and all allies in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws for 1 round.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Guangu of the Steppe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3937)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Battlecry!](/Sources.aspx?ID=268) pg. 144

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This large drum is made of hardwood and horse hide, with white silhouettes of coursing stallions along its circumference. This drum grants you a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks while playing music with the instrument.

**Activate—The Hammer of Hooves** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You hammer a thundering beat on the guangu. For 10 minutes, mounted allies within a 60-foot emanation gain a +10-foot status bonus to their mount’s Speeds. They also gain a +1 status bonus to [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) checks to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2400) and automatically succeed when they Command an Animal they’re mounted on to take a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action (such as [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305)).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Guangu of the Steppe
